purpose: complete engine-free algorithm contract for an independently implemented raw-only reproducer
allowed_runtime_inputs: shard_manifest.json identity, status and physics.npz integrity fields

manifest_layout.canonical_file: the file bytes equal UTF-8 canonical JSON with sort_keys=true, comma/colon separators, allow_nan=false and one terminal LF
manifest_layout.seal: manifest_sha256_excluding_field is exactly lowercase 64-hex SHA-256 of canonical JSON after removing only that field
manifest_layout.runtime_manifest_sha256: SHA-256 of the complete canonical manifest file including its seal field

required_paths.law_family.laws: ordered list of objects with law_id and complete spec; use the spec of the current world's law_id, including its dt
required_paths.initial_conditions: ordered list with ic_id; order is atlas order
required_paths.execution.world_ids: exact enrollment order: law order, IC order, replicate 0..R-1

manifest_layout.law_physical_fields: for each world use law_family.laws[law_id].spec.dt for cohort/activity/energy updates, spec.m_max as the closed matter upper bound, spec.n_max as the closed resource upper bound, and the universal closed bond bound [0,1]

shard_manifest_layout.canonical_file: strict finite canonical JSON under the same rule
shard_manifest_layout.world: the exact four-field world identity object from enrollment

complete_files.physics.npz: object with sha256 and bytes; reproducer must verify both before opening
complete_files.online.json: object with sha256 and bytes; reproducer must require the entry but must not open or hash the forbidden file

shard_manifest_layout.complete_physics_inventory: mapping from every NPZ array name to object {shape:[...],dtype:string}; recompute and require exact equality

failed_shard.failure_json_semantics: producer root audit requires exact identity/status, but independent reproducer must not open it; require its exact files entry and stat size only
failed_shard.optional_preserved_files: physics.npz and/or online.json may remain when failure occurred during shard verification; every immediate file except shard_manifest.json must appear exactly once in files and no unlisted file is permitted
failed_shard.independent_integrity: verify SHA-256 and bytes for any allowed physics.npz; for forbidden online.json and failure.json verify name and stat bytes without opening or hashing; emit the frozen TRACKING_UNRESOLVED failure row

shard_manifest_layout.directory_set: the immediate result-root directories equal execution.world_ids exactly; no missing, duplicate or extra shard directory

physics_layout.exact_keyset: state_step, state_m, state_n, state_b, vector_reference_max_error, deterministic_replay_equal, plus ledger__ for every array/scalar named by the raw schema
physics_layout.dtypes: state_step int64, deterministic_replay_equal uint8, all other arrays float64
physics_layout.sampling_frames: detect and track state_m frames 0 through H-1; H+1 state row is required for the final transport update but is not a detector frame
physics_layout.reference_runtime_criterion: producer compares every vectorized state element in m,n,b and every StepLedger field in dataclass order to the scalar reference using abs(error)<=1e-12+1e-10*abs(reference), and stores the maximum absolute error encountered
physics_layout.reference_raw_gate: initialize maximum_scale=1.0; in exact layout order consider state_m,state_n,state_b and every ledger__ array/scalar named by the raw schema, excluding state_step, vector_reference_max_error and deterministic_replay_equal; reject nonfinite first; update maximum_scale=max(maximum_scale,float(np.max(np.abs(array)))); require every stored vector_reference_max_error finite, nonnegative and <=1e-12+1e-10*maximum_scale

numerical_order.component_mass: math.fsum of Python float matter values in that order
numerical_order.weighted_centroid: NumPy float64 sum(lift_values*weights[:,None],axis=0) divided by component mass
numerical_order.radius: NumPy float64 row sum of squared lifted deviations, then NumPy sum(weights*squared), divide by mass, Python math.sqrt
numerical_order.periodic_distance_and_score: Python float arithmetic and math.hypot/math.exp in the written formula order
numerical_order.internal_activity: construct axis-major boolean internal-face mask; NumPy boolean-index the sum matter_forward+matter_reverse; math.fsum Python floats in resulting C order; multiply by dt then divide by dt*mass
numerical_order.internal_energy: NumPy boolean-index gross_formation_work+gross_rupture_release with the same mask; math.fsum Python floats in resulting C order; divide by dt then mass
numerical_order.cohort: NumPy float64 divide/roll/multiply/subtract operations in formula order; support/global totals use math.fsum over C-order flattened selected values
numerical_order.means: math.fsum values in ascending track-point frame order divided by valid observation count
numerical_order.comparisons: use the strict/inclusive operators written in this contract without rounding or output-derived tolerance

detector.components: periodic four-neighbour connected components; discard components with cell count below min_cells
detector.exact_traversal: unseen starts as the set of occupied row-major linear indices; choose min(unseen) as root; use a Python list as a LIFO stack; pop from the end; if popped cell is already in cells continue; otherwise add it, then visit neighbours exactly in order (-1,0),(+1,0),(0,-1),(0,+1); proposed lift is current lift plus that signed step; if occupied neighbour already has a lift compare it for winding, otherwise assign the proposed lift immediately at push time and append neighbour; after a component, subtract cells from unseen
detector.ordering: sort components by ascending minimum row-major linear cell index; component index is position after sorting
detector.winding: start the minimum-index root at its ordinary integer coordinate, propagate lifted coordinates using each von Neumann step, and mark an axis wrapped if an already lifted neighbour receives a different proposed lift on that axis
detector.mass: math.fsum matter values in sorted row-major component cells
detector.centroid: matter-weighted mean of lifted cell coordinates modulo shape; fallback unweighted mean only if mass is zero

association.dilation: repeat radius times: frontier=roll(frontier,+1/-1 on each axis) ORed together; result accumulates every frontier
association.dilated_iou: IoU of the two dilated masks
association.centroid_distance: Euclidean distance using independently minimum periodic displacement on y and x
qualification_priority: else reject if raw overlap is zero and dilated_iou is zero
selection: remove every candidate whose source or target is incident to a selected raw-overlap edge
sort remaining candidates per source by descending score then target key, and per target by descending score then source key
a top score is unique only when it is the sole candidate or exceeds the second score by strictly more than unique_score_margin
select an edge only when it is the unique top choice of both endpoints
a remaining qualified edge is ambiguity-bearing only when neither endpoint is incident to any finally selected edge

tracker.initial_tracks: one track per frame-0 component in detector order; monotonically increasing integer IDs beginning at zero
tracker.transition_graph: selected edges union ambiguity-bearing edges; process connected bipartite groups in source detector order and sorted target-key order; derive source_track_ids by mapping every source key through the current assignment and sorting the resulting integer track IDs independently of component-source iteration order
tracker.detector_collapse: a middle node with at least two selected incoming sources and two selected outgoing targets marks both adjacent groups ambiguity-bearing

resolved_cases.one_to_one: append target point to source track
resolved_cases.one_to_many: end parent and create one new child track per sorted target
resolved_cases.many_to_one: end parents and create one new merged track
resolved_cases.other_or_ambiguous: create one unresolved track per sorted target, mark every source track unresolved and emit TRACKING_UNRESOLVED

tracker.unsupported_nodes: a source with no selected/ambiguous edge dissolves; a target with no selected/ambiguous edge appears as a new track
tracker.temporary_contact: after a transition, each pair of still-distinct resolved target components whose minimum periodic cell-to-cell Manhattan distance is exactly two emits an annotation only; ancestry is unchanged
tracker.world_unresolved: any unresolved track or unresolved event

passive_cohort.enrollment: for each track independently at its first point, q=m on that detected support and q=0 elsewhere
passive_cohort.divergence: evaluate term0=(J0-roll(J0,1,axis0)) and term1=(J1-roll(J1,1,axis1)) as separate float64 arrays, then divergence=term0+term1; use the identical parenthesized order for matter_net pre/post consistency
passive_cohort.required_checks: finite float64 common shapes, finite nonnegative gross flows, pre/post matter equals pre-dt*div(matter_forward-matter_reverse), q in [0,m], and global q conservation at 1e-12+1e-10 scale
passive_cohort.turnover_at_track_point: 1 - math.fsum(q on current detected support)/initial_cohort_mass; only operation-tolerance excursions may be clipped to [0,1]

track_observation.activity_per_mass: math.fsum(dt*(matter_forward+matter_reverse) on faces with both endpoints in the support)/(dt*component_mass)
track_observation.energy_throughput_per_mass: math.fsum(gross_formation_work+gross_rupture_release on faces with both endpoints in the support)/(dt*component_mass)
track_observation.boundary crossing and support relabellin: logged diagnostics only; never internal activity or candidate input

track_metrics.integrity: one observation for every track point and no duplicate/orphan; finite ranges; area/percolation exact detector match; any integrity failure makes track and world unresolved
track_metrics.span_frames: maximum point frame minus minimum point frame plus one
track_metrics.mean_activity_per_mass: math.fsum activity divided by observed count
track_metrics.mean_energy_throughput_per_mass: math.fsum energy divided by observed count
track_metrics.post_turnover_frames: count of valid observations whose frame is strictly greater than the first frame with turnover >= min_turnover_fraction

classifier_precedence: ACTIVE_UNBOUNDED if a percolated track meets both activity and energy minima
PERCOLATED if any track ever percolates
EMPTY_OR_GAS if no component was ever detected
DISSOLVED if the final sampled frame has zero components
BOUNDED_ACTIVE_TURNOVER_CANDIDATE if any persistent track passes every area, boundedness, nonpercolation, activity, energy, turnover, post-turnover and resolution gate
PERSISTENT_NO_TURNOVER if a persistent track meets activity and energy minima but no persistent track reaches turnover minimum
STATIC_CRYSTAL_OR_SHELL if any persistent track remains
TURNOVER_WITHOUT_PERSISTENCE if a nonpersistent track reaches turnover minimum

family_aggregation.atlas: for each law in manifest order and each IC in manifest order, count every one of the nine regimes among COMPLETE worlds and preserve the enrolled denominator; per_ic.complete is the JSON boolean result of len(rows)==replicates_per_law_ic AND every row status==COMPLETE, never an integer
family_aggregation.candidate_region: an exact law_id is reproducible only when every enrolled world is COMPLETE and candidate count is at least manifest.region_rule.minimum_candidate_worlds_per_ic separately in both IC classes
disposition_precedence: REVISE_INSTRUMENTATION if any other non-COMPLETE shard

classification_output_schema.manifest_sha256: SHA-256 of the complete manifest file
classification_output_schema.worlds: lexicographic world_id list; each object has exactly world_id, law_id, ic_id, replicate, status, regime, candidate_track_ids; candidate_track_ids is sorted and is forcibly [] unless the final world regime is BOUNDED_ACTIVE_TURNOVER_CANDIDATE
classification_output_schema.atlas: manifest law order; each object exactly {region_id:law_id,law_id,per_ic,reproducible_candidate}; per_ic follows manifest IC order and each object exactly {ic_id,denominator,complete,counts}; counts has every nine-class key and integer count

output: serialize only the exact classification_output_schema with canonical JSON twice in clean processes; bytes and SHA-256 must match each other and production classification
